consit.about: A reserved constituency located at the foot of the Jawadhu Hills, defined by its tribal-adjacent population and rain-fed agriculture. It serves as a major gateway between Tiruvannamalai and the western districts of Tamil Nadu.

problem.title: Elephant-Human Conflict
problem.desc: Farmers in the hill-border villages face constant crop raids by wild elephants; solar fencing projects are incomplete, and compensation for lost life/crops is delayed by years.

problem.title: Sathanur Dam Siltation
problem.desc: The primary water source for the region has lost 30% of its capacity due to silt; water release for the 'tail-end' farmers is highly irregular, leading to crop failure.

problem.title: Lack of Cold Storage
problem.desc: As a major producer of tomatoes and seasonal vegetables, Chengam lacks a government-run cold storage unit, forcing farmers to dump produce during price crashes.

problem.title: Tribal Road Connectivity
problem.desc: Dozens of mountain hamlets still lack all-weather blacktop roads, making it impossible for ambulances to reach patients during the monsoon.

problem.title: Youth Migration
problem.desc: With no local industrial estates, nearly 40% of the rural youth migrate to Bengaluru or Chennai for low-wage construction work.

consit.about: The district headquarters and a world-renowned spiritual hub home to the Annamalaiyar Temple. It is an urbanizing constituency that balances massive seasonal pilgrim influx with a growing service-based economy.

problem.title: Girivalam Path Sanitation
problem.desc: Despite its global fame, the 14km Girivalam path struggles with open waste dumping and lack of adequate public toilets for the millions who visit during Pournami.

problem.title: Traffic & Parking Crisis
problem.desc: The town lacks a dedicated multi-level parking facility; during festival seasons, the entire city becomes a gridlock, trapping local residents in their homes.

problem.title: Underground Sewerage Failure
problem.desc: The long-pending UGD project remains partially functional, with raw sewage often overflowing into residential streets near the temple area.

problem.title: Government Medical College Gaps
problem.desc: The local Medical College is under-equipped for specialized surgeries, forcing patients to travel 90km to Vellore (CMC) for emergency trauma care.

problem.title: Unemployment in Arts Graduates
problem.desc: Despite numerous private colleges, there is no IT park or business hub, leading to high unemployment among the city's educated youth.

consit.about: A predominantly agrarian constituency situated on the eastern plains of the district. It is characterized by extensive paddy cultivation and a large network of village panchayats centered around the Kilpennathur town.

problem.title: Direct Purchase Center (DPC) Scams
problem.desc: Farmers report being forced to pay 'bribes' of ₹50 per bag to local agents at government paddy procurement centers just to offload their harvest.

problem.title: Drinking Water Salinity
problem.desc: Groundwater in several blocks has turned hard and saline; the 'Hogenakkal Phase II' water scheme has yet to reach many interior interior habitations.

problem.title: Crumbling School Buildings
problem.desc: Over 15 government primary schools in this constituency are currently operating in dilapidated structures that were officially declared unsafe in 2024.

problem.title: Milk Cooperative Arrears
problem.desc: Dairy farmers are facing a 3-month delay in payments from state-run cooperatives, making it difficult for them to purchase cattle feed.

problem.title: Encroached Irrigation Tanks
problem.desc: Major village tanks (Kanmais) are heavily encroached by local influencers, reducing their water-holding capacity and causing summer droughts.

consit.about: A scenic constituency located along the Cheyyar river basin, known for its traditional farming and unique forest-adjacent geography. It is a region with a strong rural identity and a focus on essential agriculture.

problem.title: Cheyyar River Sand Mining
problem.desc: Illegal sand mining has lowered the riverbed significantly, causing the water table in surrounding agricultural wells to drop by 200 feet in just three years.

problem.title: Lack of Emergency Care
problem.desc: The local Government Hospital lacks a 24/7 blood bank and ventilator support, requiring every emergency case to be referred to Tiruvannamalai.

problem.title: Sugar Mill Shutdown
problem.desc: The closure of nearby sugar mills has left thousands of sugarcane growers with no place to crush their crops, forcing them to switch to less profitable grains.

problem.title: Bridge Construction Delays
problem.desc: The long-promised bridge across the Cheyyar river at Polur-Kalasapakkam border remains a skeleton, forcing school kids to take 15km detours.

problem.title: High Alcoholism Rates
problem.desc: Residents report a surge in illicit liquor and 'belt shops' in rural pockets, which has led to increased domestic violence and family debt.

consit.about: A major administrative and commercial hub in the northern part of the district. It is famous for its hilltop temples and serves as a vital junction connecting Vellore, Tiruvannamalai, and Chengam.

problem.title: Vanniyar Internal Reservation
problem.desc: The 10.5% internal reservation issue is a massive talking point here; the local community feels neglected by the current government's handling of the policy.

problem.title: Interlinking of Rivers
problem.desc: The demand to interlink the Palar, Thenpennai, and Cheyyar rivers to recharge the dry groundwater table remains an unfulfilled poll promise from 2021.

problem.title: Bus Stand Encroachment
problem.desc: The main Polur bus stand is too small for the 500+ buses passing through; illegal shops on the platform make it nearly impossible for passengers to stand.

problem.title: Unemployment in Handloom
problem.desc: Traditional weavers in Polur are quitting the profession as power-looms produce cheaper imitations of their heritage designs without any GI protection.

problem.title: Stagnant SIPCOT Demand
problem.desc: There is a persistent demand for a small-scale industrial park to process local groundnut and paddy, but no land has been allotted yet.

consit.about: World-renowned as a hub for 'Arani Silk,' this constituency is an industrial and weaving powerhouse. It is a high-density region where the economy is driven by handloom silk production and rice milling.

problem.title: Silk Park Stalemate
problem.desc: The proposed 'Arani Silk Park' project, first announced in 2002, is still stuck in land acquisition and environmental clearance, killing the growth of the industry.

problem.title: Zari Price Inflation
problem.desc: The cost of pure gold/silver Zari has tripled in 2026; weavers who can't afford it are forced to use chemical Zari, which is ruining the 'Arani Silk' brand.

problem.title: Pollution from Rice Mills
problem.desc: Over 100 rice mills dump husk and untreated water into open lands, causing severe respiratory issues and groundwater contamination for nearby residents.

problem.title: Railway Connectivity
problem.desc: Arani remains isolated from the main railway network; the demand to link Arani with the Tindivanam-Nagari rail line has been ignored for decades.

problem.title: Dying Handloom Societies
problem.desc: Cooperative weaving societies are in massive debt; weavers report they haven't received government subsidies for their children's education in 2 years.

consit.about: A rapidly urbanizing constituency that hosts a massive SIPCOT industrial estate. It is a unique blend of high-tech manufacturing (footwear and electronics) and traditional river-basin agriculture.

problem.title: Land Acquisition Protests
problem.desc: Farmers are actively protesting against the 'SIPCOT Phase III' expansion, claiming the government is forcibly taking fertile multi-crop lands for factories.

problem.title: Contract Labor Exploitation
problem.desc: While factories exist, 70% of the staff are hired as contract laborers with no job security or benefits; 'local hiring' quotas are reportedly ignored.

problem.title: Groundwater Depletion
problem.desc: Massive water consumption by the SIPCOT industries has caused a severe water crisis in the surrounding 50 villages, drying up agricultural wells.

problem.title: Inadequate Housing Subsidy
problem.desc: The 'Kalaignar Kanavu Thittam' provides ₹3.5 lakh for houses, but rising material costs mean laborers need at least ₹8 lakh to finish a basic roof.

problem.title: Road Safety (ECR Link)
problem.desc: The Cheyyar-Kancheepuram road has become a 'death trap' due to heavy industrial truck traffic and a lack of proper street lighting and medians.

consit.about: A historic constituency known for the Battle of Wandiwash and its traditional Korai mat weaving industry. It is a reserved seat with a large population of landless agricultural laborers and weavers.

problem.title: Korai Mat Industry Decay
problem.desc: The traditional mat weavers of Vandavasi are being replaced by plastic mat manufacturers; there is no government subsidy for the raw Korai grass or marketing aid.

problem.title: SC Sub-Plan Fund Gaps
problem.desc: Residents in the SC colonies report that funds meant for their internal roads and street lights are being diverted to general town projects.

problem.title: Lack of Higher Education
problem.desc: There is a desperate need for a Government Arts & Science college; students currently travel 40km to Cheyyar or Madurantakam for basic degrees.

problem.title: Tail-end Irrigation Crisis
problem.desc: Farmers at the end of the Cheyyar canal system haven't seen a drop of water in three years, forcing them to leave their lands fallow.

problem.title: Unemployment in Weaving Families
problem.desc: The younger generation is fleeing Vandavasi for Chennai to work as security guards or delivery agents because the family weaving business is in debt.
